name: Pinxter Azalea
summary: Native deciduous azalea of PA acidic mesic hardwoods — the eastern wild rose of the spring forest. Fragrant pink-white tubular flowers open before the leaves in April–May, drawing emerging bumblebee queens, hummingbirds, and swallowtail butterflies.
notes: Hosts ~52 Lepidoptera species. Recently documented to depend on swallowtail butterflies as a primary pollinator (not just bees). Unlike rhododendrons, doesn't form light-blocking thickets — stays a graceful 4–6 ft loose shrub. Honest fit for Pocono acid soils. A native that finally returns spring color to the understory.

planting.site_notes: Plant at gap edges and openings beneath oaks. The pH 4.5–6.0 sweet spot exactly matches Pocono Plateau acid soils. Wire cage — deer browse moderately. Pairs with pagoda dogwood for staggered spring bloom.
